Our licensed technicians deliver fast, effective solutions to eliminate bed bugs from your home or business.

Our team uses cutting-edge thermal remediation technology and eco-friendly treatments, backed by our satisfaction guarantee and follow-up inspections to ensure your home stays bed bug-free.
localContext: From the residential neighborhoods near Cantiague Park to the apartment complexes along Old Country Road and South Broadway, Hicksville's diverse housing stock requires specialized bed bug treatment approaches. We're familiar with the unique challenges of treating both vintage Long Island homes and modern multi-family dwellings throughout the Hicksville community.

faqs.question: How much does bed bug treatment cost in Hicksville?
faqs.answer: Bed bug treatment in Hicksville typically ranges from $1,200 to $3,500 depending on the size of your home, severity of infestation, and treatment method required. We offer free inspections and provide detailed quotes before beginning any work, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How long does bed bug treatment take in Hicksville homes?
faqs.answer: Most bed bug heat treatments can be completed in 6-8 hours for an average Hicksville home. Chemical treatments may require multiple visits over 2-3 weeks. We'll provide a customized treatment plan during your free inspection based on your specific situation.

faqs.question: Do I need to leave my Hicksville home during bed bug treatment?
faqs.answer: For heat treatments, you'll need to vacate your home for 6-8 hours while we raise temperatures to eliminate bed bugs at all life stages. For chemical treatments, you may need to leave for 4-6 hours. We provide detailed preparation instructions and work around your schedule for minimal disruption.

faqs.question: Are bed bug treatments safe for families and pets in Hicksville?
faqs.answer: Yes, our bed bug treatments are completely safe when applied by our certified technicians. We use EPA-approved products and heat treatment methods that are non-toxic. Your home will be safe for children and pets to re-enter once the treatment is complete and the home has been properly ventilated.
